req.title: Run payroll
req.statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit.
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates: (1) a successful payroll run produces completed state, pay-stubs, and an audit entry (positive/EP); (2) a concurrent run attempt is rejected with 409 (negative/STC); (3) a run with exactly one active employee represents the minimum valid partition boundary (boundary/BVA); (4) a simulated internal failure must not partially commit data (negative/DT). These four cases together cover the happy path, the concurrency guard, the atomicity rollback guarantee, and the single-employee edge case, providing full traceability to all stated expected outcomes and error pathways.

tests.name: test_payroll_run_completes_with_stubs_and_audit
preconditions: At least two active employees exist with salary data
No payroll run is currently in progress
expected: Pay-stub count equals active employee count
Each stub has gross_pay > 0, deductions >= 0, net_pay == gross_pay - deductions
Audit log contains exactly one entry for this run
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_payroll_run_completes_with_stubs_and_audit(
    hr_admin_client,
    active_employees_fixture,
    payroll_month_payload,
    poll_until_completed
):
    employees = active_employees_fixture(count=2)
    resp = hr_admin_client.post("/api/payroll-runs", json=payroll_month_payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    run_id = resp.json()["id"]

    run = poll_until_completed(hr_admin_client, run_id)
    assert run["state"] == "completed"

    stubs_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/api/pay-stubs?run_id={run_id}")
    assert stubs_resp.status_code == 200
    stubs = stubs_resp.json()["items"]
    assert len(stubs) == len(employees)
    for stub in stubs:
        assert stub["gross_pay"] > 0
        assert stub["deductions"] >= 0
        assert stub["net_pay"] == pytest.approx(
            stub["gross_pay"] - stub["deductions"]
        )

    audit_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/api/audit-log?run_id={run_id}")
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    assert len(audit_resp.json()["items"]) >= 1

tests.name: test_concurrent_payroll_run_returns_409
preconditions: A payroll run for the target month is already in progress (state=running)
actions: Seed a payroll run record in state 'running' via test fixture
POST /api/payroll-runs with the same month/year payload
Inspect response status and error body
expected: Response body contains error code indicating run already in progress
trace: R-WEB-072: 409 if another run is in progress
tests.executable:
def test_concurrent_payroll_run_returns_409(
    hr_admin_client,
    in_progress_payroll_run_fixture,
    payroll_month_payload
):
    existing_run = in_progress_payroll_run_fixture(payroll_month_payload)
    assert existing_run["state"] == "running"

    resp = hr_admin_client.post("/api/payroll-runs", json=payroll_month_payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "code" in body

tests.name: test_payroll_run_with_single_active_employee
preconditions: Exactly one active employee exists with salary data
No payroll run is currently in progress
actions: Ensure only one active employee via fixture
expected: Stub arithmetic is correct: net_pay == gross_pay - deductions
trace: R-WEB-072: pay-stub records created (minimum boundary of 1 active employee)
tests.executable:
def test_payroll_run_with_single_active_employee(
    hr_admin_client,
    active_employees_fixture,
    payroll_month_payload,
    poll_until_completed
):
    employees = active_employees_fixture(count=1)
    assert len(employees) == 1

    resp = hr_admin_client.post("/api/payroll-runs", json=payroll_month_payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    run_id = resp.json()["id"]

    run = poll_until_completed(hr_admin_client, run_id)
    assert run["state"] == "completed"

    stubs_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/api/pay-stubs?run_id={run_id}")
    assert stubs_resp.status_code == 200
    stubs = stubs_resp.json()["items"]
    assert len(stubs) == 1
    stub = stubs[0]
    assert stub["net_pay"] == pytest.approx(stub["gross_pay"] - stub["deductions"])

tests.name: test_failed_payroll_run_rolls_back_atomically
preconditions: Server-side fault injection is enabled to trigger a processing failure mid-run
actions: Enable fault-injection header or config to cause mid-run failure
GET /api/pay-stubs?run_id={run_id} to verify no stubs were persisted
GET /api/audit-log?run_id={run_id} to verify failure is recorded but no committed stubs
expected: HTTP 500 or run object indicates failure
Zero pay-stub records exist for this run (rollback confirmed)
No partial data persisted for any employee
trace: R-WEB-072: 500 rolled back atomically on failure
R-WEB-072: failed run shall not partially commit
tests.executable:
def test_failed_payroll_run_rolls_back_atomically(
    hr_admin_client,
    active_employees_fixture,
    payroll_month_payload,
    fault_injection_headers,
    poll_until_terminal
):
    active_employees_fixture(count=3)

    resp = hr_admin_client.post(
        "/api/payroll-runs",
        json=payroll_month_payload,
        headers=fault_injection_headers("mid_run_failure")
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (201, 500)

    if resp.status_code == 201:
        run_id = resp.json()["id"]
        run = poll_until_terminal(hr_admin_client, run_id)
        assert run["state"] == "failed"
    else:
        run_id = resp.json().get("run_id")

    if run_id:
        stubs_resp = hr_admin_client.get(f"/api/pay-stubs?run_id={run_id}")
        assert stubs_resp.status_code == 200
        assert stubs_resp.json()["items"] == []
